entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The crop Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp. being studied for disease resistance.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A destructive biotic stress causing significant production losses in pigeonpea.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pigeonpea to resist infection by Fusarium wilt.

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The capacity of a cultivar to produce a large amount of crop.

entities.name: WIDE ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a cultivar to perform well across diverse environments.

entities.name: GENETIC CONTROL
entities.description: The genetic mechanisms that determine the expression of disease resistance.

entities.name: BREEDING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Planned approaches to develop high-yielding and resistant cultivars.

entities.name: MODE OF INHERITANCE
entities.description: The pattern by which a trait is transmitted from parents to offspring.

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATIONS
entities.description: Groups of individuals used to identify the genomic location of traits.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINES
entities.description: A specific type of mapping population used to study the inheritance of traits.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of measuring physical traits to analyze genetic control.

entities.name: MONGENIC GENE ACTION
entities.description: Inheritance of a trait controlled by a single gene, exhibiting dominant or recessive patterns.
